task_completion.description: Open and edit the Wazuh local_rules.xml file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano or vim) or a terminal command showing interaction with the file path /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml.

XML block showing rule id="100300", level="10", frequency="8", timeframe="120". It must contain <if_matched_sid>5710</if_matched_sid>, <same_source_ip /> (or similar tag), and a MITRE tag for T1110.001.

XML block showing rule id="100301", level="13", timeframe="600". It must contain <if_sid>5715</if_sid>, <if_matched_sid>100300</if_matched_sid>, <same_source_ip /> (or similar tag), and a MITRE tag for T1078.001.
XML block showing rule id="100302", level="15", timeframe="900". It must contain <if_sid>5902</if_sid>, <if_matched_sid>100301</if_matched_sid>, and a MITRE tag for T1136.001.

task_completion.description: Verify the rules are active via the Wazuh API
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a curl command or script making an HTTP request to the Wazuh API (typically port 55000) querying the /rules endpoint for the newly created rule IDs.

task_completion.description: Save the API response to the specified JSON file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing output redirection (e.g., > /home/ga/correlated_rules_verification.json) or a command confirming the JSON response was written to /home/ga/correlated_rules_verification.json.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually queried the Wazuh API and didn't mock the response
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine API interaction commands (like curl with authentication headers/tokens) rather than echo commands manually constructing a fake JSON string.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent edited the actual Wazuh rules file, not a local copy
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the file being edited is exactly /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml and not a dummy file in the user's home directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the saved JSON file contains a realistic Wazuh API response structure
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of correlated_rules_verification.json (if visible) should show standard Wazuh API response wrappers like 'data', 'affected_items', and 'error' fields, not just a raw list of rules.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target rules file path: /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. Target verification file: /home/ga/correlated_rules_verification.json. Required Rule 100300: level 10, frequency 8, timeframe 120, if_matched_sid 5710, mitre T1110.001. Required Rule 100301: level 13, if_sid 5715, if_matched_sid 100300, timeframe 600, mitre T1078.001. Required Rule 100302: level 15, if_sid 5902, if_matched_sid 100301, timeframe 900, mitre T1136.001.
